facts.shortSummary: Robin takes possession of Asineau, and the party begins rebuilding the village into a stronger, wealthier, and more welcoming home base.
facts.longSummary: After the fallen Baroness is convicted of treason and Robin accepts the lordship of Asineau, the party throws itself into remaking the village. Robin focuses on defenses, Celyn on the local economy, and Izgil on research and craft infrastructure, while new smithing, workshop, garden, and glassmaking projects begin to take shape. The village draws in craftsmen, opens trade with the nearby lizardfolk, hosts festivals and halfling merchants, and starts to settle into a more confident and prosperous life under its new lord.

location.context: Robin's new demesne and the village works being expanded around it
location.notes: The beat is a broad civic montage centered on the party's new base.

npcs.name: Lorin Valbert
npcs.notes: His neglect and taxation left Asineau depleted before Robin took over.

npcs.name: Odo Cordwaner
npcs.notes: Referenced as the party's ally whose brother will be trained in the new smithy.

npcs.name: Brot Starsearcher
npcs.notes: Associated with the new workshop and the push for lens and glass production.

npcs.name: the miller
npcs.notes: Celyn keeps an eye on him because of his hostility toward lizardfolk.

facts.shortSummary: Vahaiya brings news of a marauding dragon, and the party rides east to Marwick to assess the threat and volunteer to face it.
facts.longSummary: In high summer, Vahaiya warns the party that a dragon is ravaging the border between Tyrwingha and Addermarch and may be linked to an older, more terrible draconic threat from the Great War. With local rulers short on troops and the broader southern crisis tying down the Sembaran response, the party decides to intervene. They travel east to Marwick, where they find a half-emptied stronghold, learn how the dragon has been destroying the summer villages, and confirm that it is dangerous but not the ancient terror Vahaiya feared.

location.context: eastward travel to the castle nearest the dragon's attacks
location.notes: The beat ends with the party established at Marwick and briefed on the threat.

npcs.name: Vahaiya
npcs.notes: Brings the warning and recognizes the danger of a related dragon line.

npcs.name: the Earl of Marwick
npcs.notes: Has already withdrawn non-essential people from the vulnerable uplands.

organizations.name: Sembaran Army
organizations.notes: Much of the army has been disrupted or pulled south, leaving little force to answer the dragon.

organizations.name: The Rangers
organizations.notes: They are also occupied by the elemental ash crisis in the south.

facts.shortSummary: Robin designs a ballista for the dragon while Celyn and Viepuck scout the ruined villages, find kobold traces, and pin down the beast's habits.
facts.longSummary: At Marwick, Robin applies his new interest in siegecraft to the problem of grounding a fast-moving dragon, laying out a specialized ballista while the others gather field intelligence. Celyn, Viepuck, and one of the Earl's surviving knights search the destroyed settlements, discover signs that kobolds are active around the lair, and spend the next day watching the dragon's movements from the ridge. Once they confirm when it leaves to hunt and plunder, the party returns to finish the weapon and prepare an ambush.

location.context: scouting the ravaged uplands and observing the dragon from the ridge
location.notes: The beat moves between the castle, the ruined villages, and the lookout above them.

npcs.name: the Earl's surviving knight
npcs.notes: One of the few knights left to help the party investigate the uplands.

facts.shortSummary: The party intercepts a kobold foraging group, talks them into surrender, and turns their grudging testimony into an ambush plan for the dragon.
facts.longSummary: Using cows and the concealed ballista as bait, the party waits in a ruined village and catches a small kobold group sneaking in ahead of the dragon. Viepuck and Celyn help force a surrender, and the party teases out a surprising amount of information from the captives: the kobolds were driven north from the south by the same ash-cloud disaster now unsettling the realm, their dragon commands several rival factions, and not all of them are eager to die for its glory. By the end of the conversation Robin has persuaded the least enthusiastic kobolds to stay nearby and support a trap when the dragon returns.

location.primary: ruined mountain village near the dragon's lair
location.context: the baited ambush site with concealed ballista and cow decoy
location.notes: Most of the beat is a tense interrogation scene at the ruined village.

npcs.name: the winged kobold leader
npcs.notes: Talkative, proud of her wings, and more pragmatic than openly loyal.

npcs.name: the winged kobold's sister
npcs.notes: More patient and less impressed by her sister's authority.

npcs.name: Vahaiya
npcs.notes: The kobolds confirm that the feared southern dragon was reportedly destroyed by an ash-cloud horror.

facts.shortSummary: With the trap reset and the allied kobolds in place, the party waits outside the lair until a midnight warning announces the dragon's approach.
facts.longSummary: After settling on the details of the ambush, the party sends Es*tiaslos up near the lair to watch for movement and takes positions in the valley. Robin also begins imagining what might happen if the surviving kobolds were brought back to Asineau, a prospect that delights Celyn mostly because of the local uproar it would cause. The quiet ends when the warning comes in after midnight: the dragon is on the move with five kobolds and is heading straight toward them.

location.context: the concealed overnight ambush position outside the lair approach
location.notes: The beat is a short night watch immediately before open combat.
